I struggle to stay consistent with home workouts

Consistency is a systems problem, not a willpower problem. Research consistently shows that habit formation requires reducing friction — making the desired behavior easier than the alternative.

For home workouts specifically:
📋 Same time every day (habit stacking)
👟 Workout clothes ready the night before
📱 Open Luvit before sitting down after work
⏱️ Commit to just 10 minutes — momentum usually carries you further

What's the most common reason you skip? That tells us exactly what system to fix.

How do I warm up properly before a home workout?

A proper warm-up prepares the nervous system, increases blood flow to muscles, and significantly reduces injury risk. For home workouts, 5-7 minutes is sufficient:

🔄 Joint circles: ankles, knees, hips, shoulders, wrists (30 sec each)
🚶 Marching in place: 60 seconds
🦵 Leg swings: 10 each direction, each leg
💪 Arm circles: 30 seconds
🏃 Light movement: jumping jacks or high knees, 60 seconds

Then 2-3 easy reps of your first exercise before going to working weight/intensity. Never skip this — cold muscles are more injury-prone. The Luvit app's warm-up routines are built into each workout automatically.

Can resistance bands replace dumbbells for home workouts?

For most training goals, yes — resistance bands are highly effective and in some ways superior:

✅ Constant tension throughout the movement (unlike free weights)
✅ Joint-friendly — lower injury risk
✅ Versatile — can replicate most dumbbell exercises
✅ Progressive — bands come in different resistances

Band equivalents:
💪 Dumbbell curl → Band curl
💪 Dumbbell row → Band row
💪 Shoulder press → Band overhead press
💪 Deadlift → Band deadlift

The main limitation: very heavy compound loading (like heavy squats) is harder to replicate. But for hypertrophy and fitness, bands are excellent. I recommend the 'Full Body Resistance Bands' programme in the Luvit library.

Should I do cardio or strength training at home?

Both. Always both. But the ratio depends on your goal:

🎯 Fat loss: 60% strength, 40% cardio
🎯 Muscle building: 80% strength, 20% cardio
🎯 General fitness: 50/50

Strength training builds muscle which burns more calories at rest. Cardio burns calories during the session. Combined is optimal. Don't skip either. What's your goal?

All doable at home, zero equipment. But sort your diet first or you'll never see them. Check out 'Core Crusher Home' in the Luvit library. What does your diet look like?

How do I progress bodyweight workouts without adding weight?

Great question — progression without equipment follows specific principles:

📈 Increase reps or sets
📈 Slow down the movement (3 sec down, pause, 1 sec up)
📈 Reduce rest time between sets
📈 Advance to harder variations:
  → Push-ups → Decline push-ups → Archer push-ups → One-arm
  → Squats → Bulgarian split squats → Pistol squats
  → Planks → Plank shoulder taps → RKC plank

This is called 'skill-based progression' and provides months to years of challenge with zero equipment. The Luvit library has progression guides for each movement — I recommend starting there.

What should I eat before a home workout?

Pre-workout fuel is so important! 🌟 The simple rule: eat something with carbs + protein about 60-90 min before training.

Easy home options:
🍌 Banana + peanut butter (my favourite!)
🍞 Toast with eggs
🥛 Oats with some fruit
🧃 Smoothie with banana + yoghurt

And drink water! Being dehydrated makes workouts SO much harder. Do you usually eat before training? 😊

I work from home and sit all day, what should I do?

Prolonged sitting is genuinely problematic for health — it tightens hip flexors, weakens glutes, and strains the lower back. The good news: working from home gives you unique opportunities to move.

Practical strategies:
🕐 Movement breaks every 45-60 minutes (set a timer)
💪 5-min desk exercises: chair squats, standing hip hinges, shoulder rolls
🦵 After every meeting: 10 bodyweight squats
🚶 Walking calls — walk around your home during phone meetings

For your formal training: prioritise hip flexor stretching, glute activation, and thoracic mobility. I recommend 'Desk Worker Recovery' in the Luvit library — it directly addresses sitting posture damage.

What's the minimum equipment I should buy for home workouts?

For maximum value, in order of priority:

1️⃣ Resistance band set (~€15-30) — adds resistance to every exercise
2️⃣ Pull-up bar for doorframe (~€20-30) — unlocks back and bicep training
3️⃣ A pair of adjustable dumbbells (~€50-100) — opens full programme variety

Total investment: ~€85-160 for a complete home gym. A gym membership costs that every 2-3 months. You can achieve excellent results with just the bands alone while you save up for more.

I only have 20 minutes a day, is that enough?

20 minutes is PERFECT for a beginner! 🌟 Honestly, 20 focused minutes beats a 1-hour unfocused workout every time. Here's a quick daily routine:

⚡ 5 min warm-up (marching, arm circles)
⚡ 12 min workout (3 exercises × 4 rounds)
⚡ 3 min cool-down stretch

Check out '20-Minute Home Express' in the Luvit library — it's designed exactly for this! Consistency with 20 minutes will absolutely change your body 💪
What exercises can I do in 20 minutes?

Here's a great 20-min no-equipment routine:

🌟 Round 1-4 (45 sec on / 15 sec rest):
1. Squats
2. Push-ups (or knee push-ups)
3. Reverse lunges

That's it — simple, effective, done! Add 1 rep each week and you'll be amazed how quickly you progress. Check out 'Beginner 20-Min' in the Luvit library! 🎉

My wrists hurt when I do push-ups

Wrist pain during push-ups is very common. A few causes and solutions:

1. Wrist flexibility — warm up wrists with circles before training
2. Weight distribution — spread fingers wide, press through knuckles
3. Try fist push-ups — keeps wrists neutral (safer for many people)
4. Push-up handles or dumbbells on floor — neutral wrist position

If pain is sharp or persists, please consult a physiotherapist. In the meantime, switch to incline push-ups on a wall or elevated surface — much less wrist load. Would you like alternatives?

How do I do squats correctly at home?

THE most important home exercise — get it right. Stand feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Push hips BACK first, then down — like sitting in a chair behind you. Chest up, knees tracking over toes. Go as deep as mobility allows — thighs parallel minimum.

Biggest mistake: knees caving inward. If yours do, put a resistance band just above knees — forces them out.

How do I do a proper push-up at home?

The push-up is one of the most effective home exercises when done correctly. Key form points:

📐 Hand position: slightly wider than shoulder-width, fingers spread
📐 Body: rigid straight line from head to heels — no sagging hips
📐 Elbow angle: about 45° from body (not flared out wide)
📐 Range: chest touches or approaches the floor
📐 Breathing: inhale down, exhale up

If full push-ups are too difficult: incline push-ups (hands on sofa/wall) are a legitimate progression, not a failure. They build the same muscles. I recommend 'Push-Up Masterclass' in the Luvit library — it covers all variations from beginner to advanced.

I work from home and sit all day, what should I do?

Prolonged sitting is genuinely problematic for health — it tightens hip flexors, weakens glutes, and strains the lower back. The good news: working from home gives you unique opportunities to move.

Practical strategies:
🕐 Movement breaks every 45-60 minutes (set a timer)
💪 5-min desk exercises: chair squats, standing hip hinges, shoulder rolls
🦵 After every meeting: 10 bodyweight squats
🚶 Walking calls — walk around your home during phone meetings

For your formal training: prioritise hip flexor stretching, glute activation, and thoracic mobility. I recommend 'Desk Worker Recovery' in the Luvit library — it directly addresses sitting posture damage.

How do I do a proper push-up at home?

The push-up is one of the most effective home exercises when done correctly. Key form points:

📐 Hand position: slightly wider than shoulder-width, fingers spread
📐 Body: rigid straight line from head to heels — no sagging hips
📐 Elbow angle: about 45° from body (not flared out wide)
📐 Range: chest touches or approaches the floor
📐 Breathing: inhale down, exhale up

If full push-ups are too difficult: incline push-ups (hands on sofa/wall) are a legitimate progression, not a failure. They build the same muscles. I recommend 'Push-Up Masterclass' in the Luvit library — it covers all variations from beginner to advanced.

I have lower back pain, what home exercises are safe?
Lower back pain is very common and most cases respond well to the right movement. First — is this a diagnosed condition or general tightness?
Just tightness, no diagnosis

Muscular tightness often responds well to targeted exercise. For home training:

Avoid: sit-ups, crunches, heavy loaded spinal flexion

Focus on:
🧘 Cat-cow stretches — gentle spinal mobility
💪 Glute bridges — strong glutes relieve back load
💪 Bird-dog — core stability without spinal stress
💪 Dead bug — excellent safe core work

I recommend 'Lower Back Relief' in the Luvit library. If pain persists beyond 2 weeks, please see a physiotherapist.
What about deadlifts with a resistance band?

Band Romanian deadlifts are actually excellent for lower back recovery when done correctly — they strengthen the posterior chain (glutes, hamstrings) which directly supports the lower back.

Key form points: hinge at hips (not waist), neutral spine throughout, push hips back not down. Start very light and stop if you feel any sharp pain. This is a therapeutic movement when done right.

How do I do squats correctly at home?

THE most important home exercise — get it right. Stand feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Push hips BACK first, then down — like sitting in a chair behind you. Chest up, knees tracking over toes. Go as deep as mobility allows — thighs parallel minimum.

Biggest mistake: knees caving inward. If yours do, put a resistance band just above knees — forces them out.

Check out 'Perfect Squat' in the Luvit library before leg day. How deep can you currently go?

I snack too much because I'm home all day

Classic home problem — the kitchen is always there. Here's the fix:

1️⃣ Don't buy junk — can't eat what isn't there
2️⃣ Prep protein snacks — Greek yoghurt, boiled eggs, cottage cheese — ready in the fridge
3️⃣ Drink water first — often 'hungry' is actually thirst
4️⃣ Brush your teeth after meals — psychological signal that eating is done
5️⃣ Leave the kitchen — don't hang around where food is

What are you usually reaching for?
Chips and biscuits mostly

Swap chips for rice cakes — same crunch, won't destroy your deficit. Biscuits → dark chocolate 85%, 2-3 squares. Same satisfaction, way less damage. Small environment changes beat willpower every time. 💪

You CAN train upper body and core at home while it heals. After 72 hours, gentle walking. Light leg work after 5-7 days only.

If you heard a pop or can't walk normally — see a doctor immediately. Don't rush this one.

Can resistance bands replace dumbbells for home workouts?

For most training goals, yes — resistance bands are highly effective and in some ways superior:

✅ Constant tension throughout the movement (unlike free weights)
✅ Joint-friendly — lower injury risk
✅ Versatile — can replicate most dumbbell exercises
✅ Progressive — bands come in different resistances

Band equivalents:
💪 Dumbbell curl → Band curl
💪 Dumbbell row → Band row
💪 Shoulder press → Band overhead press
💪 Deadlift → Band deadlift

The main limitation: very heavy compound loading (like heavy squats) is harder to replicate. But for hypertrophy and fitness, bands are excellent. I recommend the 'Full Body Resistance Bands' programme in the Luvit library.

How do I warm up properly before a home workout?

A proper warm-up prepares the nervous system, increases blood flow to muscles, and significantly reduces injury risk. For home workouts, 5-7 minutes is sufficient:

🔄 Joint circles: ankles, knees, hips, shoulders, wrists (30 sec each)
🚶 Marching in place: 60 seconds
🦵 Leg swings: 10 each direction, each leg
💪 Arm circles: 30 seconds
🏃 Light movement: jumping jacks or high knees, 60 seconds

Then 2-3 easy reps of your first exercise before going to working weight/intensity. Never skip this — cold muscles are more injury-prone. The Luvit app's warm-up routines are built into each workout automatically.

What should I eat to support home workouts?

Nutrition principles are the same regardless of where you train. The fundamentals:

🥩 Protein: 1.6-2g per kg bodyweight (muscle repair and building)
🍚 Carbs: fuel your sessions — eat them around workout time
🥑 Fats: essential for hormones — don't cut them too low
💧 Hydration: even mild dehydration reduces performance significantly

For home workouts specifically — since you're not burning extra calories commuting to a gym, be mindful of total intake. What are your current goals — fat loss, muscle gain, or maintenance?

How do I progress bodyweight workouts without adding weight?

Great question — progression without equipment follows specific principles:

📈 Increase reps or sets
📈 Slow down the movement (3 sec down, pause, 1 sec up)
📈 Reduce rest time between sets
📈 Advance to harder variations:
  → Push-ups → Decline push-ups → Archer push-ups → One-arm
  → Squats → Bulgarian split squats → Pistol squats
  → Planks → Plank shoulder taps → RKC plank

This is called 'skill-based progression' and provides months to years of challenge with zero equipment. The Luvit library has progression guides for each movement — I recommend starting there.
